thag16.8:1.1:
Aṅgulimāla:
“While walking, contemplative, you say,
’I have stopped.’
thag16.8:1.2: But when I have stopped you say that I haven’t.
thag16.8:1.3: I ask you the meaning of this:
thag16.8:1.4:
How have you stopped?
How haven’t I?”
The Buddha:
thag16.8:2.1: “I have stopped, Aṅgulimāla, once & for all,
thag16.8:2.2: having cast off violence toward all living beings.
thag16.8:2.3: You, though, are unrestrained toward beings.
thag16.8:2.4:
That’s how I’ve stopped and you haven’t.”
Aṅgulimāla:
thag16.8:3.1: “At long last a greatly revered great seer
thag16.8:3.2: for my sake has come to the great forest.
thag16.8:3.3: Having heard your verse in line with the Dhamma,
thag16.8:3.4: I will go about having abandoned evil.”
thag16.8:4.1: So saying, the bandit hurled his sword & weapons
thag16.8:4.2: over a cliff into a chasm, a pit.
thag16.8:4.3: Then the bandit paid homage to the feet of the One Well-Gone,
thag16.8:4.4: and right there requested the Going-forth.
thag16.8:5.1: The Awakened One, the compassionate great seer,
thag16.8:5.2: the teacher of the world, along with its devas,
thag16.8:5.4: That in itself was monkhood for him. * * * Aṅgulimāla:
thag16.8:6.4: like the moon set free from a cloud.
thag16.8:7.4: like the moon set free from a cloud.
thag16.8:8.2: devotes himself to the Buddha’s bidding:
thag16.8:8.4: like the moon set free from a cloud.
thag16.8:9.1: May even my enemies hear talk of the Dhamma.
thag16.8:9.2: May even my enemies devote themselves to the Buddha’s bidding.
thag16.8:9.3: May even my enemies associate with those people who—peaceful, good—
thag16.8:9.4: get others to accept the Dhamma.
thag16.8:10.1: May even my enemies hear the Dhamma time & again
thag16.8:10.2: from those who advise endurance, forbearance,
thag16.8:11.1: For surely he wouldn’t harm me,
thag16.8:11.3: he would attain the foremost peace,
thag16.8:13.1: Some tame with a blunt stick,
thag16.8:13.3: But without blunt or bladed weapons
thag16.8:13.4: I was tamed by the one who is Such.
thag16.8:14.1: “Doer of No Harm” is my name,
thag16.8:14.2: but I used to be a doer of harm.
thag16.8:14.3: Today I am true to my name,
thag16.8:14.4: for I harm no one at all.
thag16.8:15.1: A bandit I used to be,
thag16.8:15.3: Swept along by a great flood,
thag16.8:15.4: I went to the Buddha as refuge.
thag16.8:16.4: Uprooted is [craving], the guide to becoming.
thag16.8:17.1: Having done the type of kamma
thag16.8:17.2: that would lead to many bad destinations,
thag16.8:17.3: touched by the fruit of [that] kamma,
thag16.8:18.3: while one who is wise cherishes heedfulness
thag16.8:19.2: or to intimacy with sensual delight—
thag16.8:19.3: for a heedful person, absorbed in jhāna,
thag16.8:20.1: This has come well & not gone away,
thag16.8:20.2: it was not badly thought through for me.
thag16.8:21.1: This has come well & not gone away,
thag16.8:21.2: it was not badly thought through for me.
thag16.8:21.3: The three knowledges have been attained;
thag16.8:22.1: Where once I stayed here & there
thag16.8:22.3: in the wilderness, at the foot of a tree,
thag16.8:23.1: with ease I now lie down, I stand,
thag16.8:23.2: with ease I live my life.
thag16.8:23.4: O, the Teacher has shown me sympathy!
thag16.8:24.1: Before, I was of brahman stock,
thag16.8:24.3: Today I’m the son of the One Well-Gone,
thag16.8:25.1: Rid of craving, devoid of clinging,
thag16.8:25.3: having killed the root of evil,
thag16.8:25.4: I’ve reached the end of the effluents.
thag16.8:26.1: The Teacher has been served by me;
